content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Working-tree `git diff` was empty, so I reviewed the branch delta against `origin/master`, read the prompt-contract generator and consumer changes end-to-end, and ran targeted cargo tests around `task_prompt_contract`, `prompt_builder`, and `project_create_from_bead`. I found 3 correctness gaps.

## Amendments

1. `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:377-385` only validates a bead prompt override when the override already contains the contract marker. A caller can still use `project create-from-bead --prompt-file` with arbitrary markdown and create a bead-backed task whose `prompt.md` has no contract marker and none of the required sections; `tests/unit/project_run_record_test.rs:917-945` now locks that behavior in. Every downstream consumer added in this change gates its extra guidance on `prompt_uses_contract(...)`, so this supported path silently opts milestone/bead execution out of the new canonical contract.
   Rationale: This leaves a first-class bead execution path without the promised shared prompt shape or version marker, so contract-driven behavior and drift detection are inconsistent.
2. `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:160-166` stop flagging unexpected canonical headings once `expected_index` reaches the end of the section list. That means a rewritten prompt can append a second `## Must-Do Scope`, `## Acceptance Criteria`, etc. after `## AGENTS / Repo Guidance` and still pass `validate_canonical_prompt_shape`; the permissive test at `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:279-285` bakes that in. Because prompt review uses this validator as its acceptance gate, the checker no longer guarantees one unambiguous authoritative section set.
   Rationale: The contract validator is supposed to prevent prompt drift. Allowing duplicate canonical sections after the last heading undermines the 'one clear contract' guarantee and can hand downstream stages conflicting scope instructions.
3. `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:568-581` exit `NonGoals`/`AcceptanceCriteria` mode on any markdown heading, not just another canonical section. A bead description like `Non-goals:\n### Examples\n- do not change CLI` will reset `active_section` at `### Examples`, so the later bullets fall back into `Must-Do Scope` and become in-scope work in the rendered prompt. The added coverage at `tests/unit/project_run_record_test.rs:804-845` only exercises plain subsection labels, so this markdown-heading case is still untested.
   Rationale: Bead descriptions in this repo already use markdown headings heavily. Misclassifying those bullets changes the execution boundary reviewers and implementers see.
